Coding bootcamp
triples.object: Coding bootcamps are intensive programs of software development . They first appeared in 2011. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Computer science
Software engineering
triples.object: Software engineering is a branch of both computer science and engineering focused on designing, developing, testing, and maintaining software applications . [ 1 ] It involves applying engineering principles and computer programming expertise to develop software systems that meet user needs. [ 2 ] [

triples.subject: Massive open online course
Apprenticeship
triples.object: Apprenticeship is a system for training potential new practitioners of a trade or profession with on-the-job training and often some accompanying study. Apprenticeships may also enable practitioners to gain a license to practice in a regulated occupation. Most of their training is done while working

triples.subject: Self-taught
Income share agreement
triples.object: An income share agreement (or ISA ) is a financial structure in which an individual or organization provides something of value (often a fixed amount of money) to a recipient who, in exchange, agrees to pay back a percentage of their income for a fixed number of years.

triples.subject: Cost of attendance
Computer programming
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
triples.object: A Bachelor of Science ( BS , BSc , SB , or ScB ; from the Latin scientiae baccalaureus ) [ 1 ] is a bachelor's degree awarded upon the completion and fulfillment of the requirements of an undergraduate program . [ 2 ]

triples.subject: App Academy
triples.object: App Academy is a San Francisco based coding bootcamp founded by Ned Ruggeri and Kush Patel in 2012.

triples.subject: Hack Reactor
triples.object: Hack Reactor is a software engineering coding bootcamp [ 2 ] education program founded in San Francisco in 2012. [ 3 ] The program is remote-only and offered in 12-week beginner full-time and 19-week intermediate full-time formats.

triples.subject: Lambda School
Coursera
triples.object: Coursera Inc. ( / k Ér Ë s É r É / ) is an American for-profit online university, earlier operating as a global massive open online course provider from 2012 until 2021. It was founded in 2012 [ 2 ] [ 3 ] by Stanford University computer science professors Andrew Ng and Daphne Koller . [ 4 ] Cour

triples.subject: Udacity
triples.object: Udacity, Inc. is an American global for-profit massive open online course provider. It was founded by Sebastian Thrun , David Stavens , and Mike Sokolsky. [ 4 ] [ 5 ] [ 6 ]

triples.subject: General Assembly (school)
Multiverse (company)
Internship
triples.object: An internship is a period of work experience offered by an organization for a limited period of time. [ 1 ] Once confined to medical graduates, internship is used to practice for a wide range of placements in businesses, non-profit organizations , government agencies and political parties. They are

triples.subject: Job interview
triples.object: A job interview is an interview consisting of a conversation between a job applicant and a representative of an employer which is conducted to assess whether the applicant should be hired. [ 1 ] Interviews are one of the most common methods of employee selection. [ 1 ] Interviews vary in the extent

triples.subject: Algorithm
Data structure
triples.object: In computer science , a data structure is a data organization and storage format that is usually chosen for efficient access to data. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] More precisely, a data structure is a collection of data values, the relationships among them, and the functions or operations that can be applied t

triples.subject: GitHub
Stack Overflow
Front-end web development
triples.object: Front-end web development is the development of the graphical user interface of a website through the use of HTML , CSS , and JavaScript so users can view and interact with that website. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ]

triples.subject: DevOps
